title: Knowledge Representation and Reasoning
description: How AI systems represent facts and rules, and how forward and backward chaining derive new conclusions from a knowledge base.
objectives: Represent simple facts and if-then rules using predicate-style notation
Explain how a rule-based (expert) system combines a knowledge base with an inference engine to answer questions
Trace forward chaining step-by-step, deriving new facts in order until the goal is reached or no more rules apply
Trace backward chaining step-by-step, reducing a goal to subgoals and checking them against known facts
Compare forward and backward chaining and judge which is more efficient for a given situation
Implement a small forward-chaining inference loop in Python

lessons.title: Representing knowledge as facts and rules
Forward chaining: reasoning from facts to conclusions
Backward chaining and comparing the two strategies
